stemHtml: Prior to every Commercial Air Transport flight of a large aircraft, the Operator uses an electronic or a paper format trim sheet to determine the Centre of Gravity of the actual Zero Fuel Mass, Take-Off Mass and Landing Mass. What is the purpose of the trim sheet?

options.a: This is to check that they are below the limits published in the Aircraft flight Manual, and is achieved by calculating the moments.
options.b: This is to ensure that the CGs are within the certified envelope, and is achieved by using the index method.
options.c: This is done by referring to the stations of various fuselage sectors defined in mass and balance documentation.
options.d: This is done by computing the balance arm of the actual operational masses and the results are given in metres or inches.
